pages.raw_text:
Drivers of express wagons have
given the office of City Comptroller
Farley the appearance of pension
day at the county clerks office the
last two days. The time for the
annual renewal of licenses is at
hand and each of the nearly 400 ex-
press wagons of the city must en-
rich the city treasury to the amount
of 81.


The government will expend
$500 in giving an exhibit of the
products of dry farming at the
next Dry Farming congress, to be
held in Spokane in October. The
government will aim to have every
dry farming experiment station rep
resented.


The store of the Nance Millinery
Co., at Lincoln street, was badly
damaged by water from a broken
main in the Denver hotel, on the
upper floors, last night.


The Northern Pacific has issued

tied TO stop at Standpoint when

derwent an operation for appeudi-
citis last night at st. Lukes hos
pital.


A. M. Luofor, chief engineer of
the Inland Empire system, has gone
to the town of La Crosse, in Whit
man county to oversee the survey
ing being done for the new extern
sion of the Colfax line to the Snake
river. The company is DESTROYS of
